what_it_does: A standalone acceptance test for the finding-disposition policy. It creates a temporary FsStore, appends one structural finding, then exercises cd.dispose_finding to verify that agent-disposable dispositions (to_wire, resolved) can be applied by the loop, that by-design is escalated rather than applied unless an operator supplies confirmed=True, and that unknown dispositions are rejected fail-loud.
observations: The module docstring frames the test around C4: findings are agent-disposable, but by-design escalates through a consent floor.
A simple check(label, cond) helper asserts conditions and increments a global PASS counter.
It inserts the project root into sys.path and imports FsStore and runtime.coherence_detect.
It creates a temp directory and FsStore, appends an unwired-route finding, and runs five disposition checks.
It verifies that an agent by-design request is ok but not applied and is escalated.
It verifies that an operator-confirmed by-design request is applied and persisted.
It verifies that a bogus disposition is refused (not ok, not applied).
standouts: The test explicitly encodes a consent boundary: agents may not self-accept by-design gaps.
It treats by-design as a micro-ADR requiring operator confirmation.
It asserts both negative and positive paths for escalation and confirmation.
conventions: Uses a lightweight inline test harness rather than pytest/unittest.
Uses a global mutable PASS counter.
Uses sys.path manipulation to import project modules.
Uses a temporary directory for an isolated store.
concerns: The check helper mixes assertion with side-effect counting, so failures abort the script before the final summary.
No explicit cleanup of the temporary directory is shown.
The test relies on external behavior of cd.dispose_finding and FsStore that is not defined in this file.
The global PASS variable is mutated from inside a function.
notes: A policy acceptance script that pins the boundary between agent-disposable findings and operator-consent by-design acceptance.
questions: What is the complete catalog of agent-disposable dispositions beyond to_wire and resolved?
How does the escalation surface to the operator inbox or consent floor?
What fields identify an operator versus the loop in dispose_finding?
What is the schema and lifecycle of dispositions.jsonl?
How are confirmed operator decisions persisted or audited?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it tests agent-disposable dispositions, by-design escalation, operator confirmation, and fail-loud unknown dispositions.
apparent_themes: Agent autonomy limited to routine dispositions
Separation of disposition lane from operator inbox
intention_signals: To encode the rule that routine findings can be burned down automatically, but permanently accepting a gap as by-design is a consequential operator decision that must pass a confirmation gate.
novelty: It tests the non-application of an otherwise valid disposition when the caller lacks confirmation authority, making the consent boundary explicit and executable.
contribution: A behavioral contract test that records the intended agent/operator boundary for finding disposition and provides a concrete example of how by-design acceptance requires operator confirmation.
summary_for_embedding: A standalone acceptance test for finding-disposition policy that verifies agent-disposable dispositions, by-design escalation without operator confirmation, and fail-loud rejection of unknown dispositions.
intention_for_embedding: To lock in the C4 rule that routine findings are agent-disposable while by-design acceptance requires operator consent.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments a global pass counter, and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: A minimal inline test helper used by the acceptance script. It takes a label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with the label in the failure message, increments the global PASS counter on success, and prints a formatted ok message.
